name: mouse_iap_erv
description: Intracisternal A-Particle (IAP): a mouse-specific endogenous retrovirus family (LTR retroelement) with ~1000-2000 active or recently-active copies in the genome plus thousands of solo-LTRs. Full element is ~7 kb with ~350 bp LTRs (5' and 3' identical at the time of integration). IAPs are unusually transcriptionally active for an ERV; they are the leading cause of spontaneous de-novo insertion mutations in laboratory mice (responsible for ~10-15% of new mutant alleles). Recent activity makes them a major source of strain-specific structural variation.
